title: Good gh-ost invocation — correct tool usage for large table type change
migration_snippet:
# Migration plan for changing user_id INT → BIGINT on events table (80M rows)
# Step 1: dry-run
gh-ost --host=replica-host --allow-on-master \
  --database=app --table=events \
  --alter='MODIFY COLUMN user_id BIGINT NOT NULL' \
  --chunk-size=1000 --max-lag-millis=1500 \
  --throttle-control-replicas='replica1:3306,replica2:3306' \
  --exact-rowcount --concurrent-rowcount \
  --initially-drop-ghost-table --initially-drop-old-table

# Step 2: execute (add --execute)
# Step 3: verify
# SELECT COLUMN_TYPE FROM information_schema.COLUMNS WHERE TABLE_NAME='events' AND COLUMN_NAME='user_id';
expected_feedback: No violations. Correct use of gh-ost for a COPY-requiring operation (INT→BIGINT) on a large table. Dry-run first, replica-based binlog reading, throttle controls, exact row count for ETA. Type change requires COPY which is unsafe at 80M rows — gh-ost is the right tool choice.
